evals.name: Suno.com capture planning
evals.prompt: Plan the complete traffic capture for https://suno.com. Include exact playwright-cli commands for site assessment (framework detection, protection checks, API probe), the full exploration checklist (READ + WRITE), and structured findings.

assertions.text: Produces structured findings (architecture, API surface, protections, strategy)
Has specific eval commands for framework detection
Mentions Force SPA Navigation trick by name and explains why
Recommends specific capture strategy with rationale
Maps strategy to CLI generation impact

evals.name: Next.js SSR site capture
evals.prompt: Plan traffic capture for https://vercel.com/dashboard (a Next.js app). Include __NEXT_DATA__ extraction, Force SPA Navigation trick, and SSR+API hybrid strategy.

evals.name: Protected site capture (Futbin)
evals.prompt: Plan traffic capture for https://www.futbin.com. Check for Cloudflare, rate limits, and recommend a capture strategy considering protections.

evals.name: Full pipeline planning for Suno
evals.prompt: Plan the complete implementation of cli-web-suno for https://suno.com. Cover all phases: capture, analyze, design, implement with parallel subagents, test planning, test writing, documentation, publish with smoke test.

assertions.text: Has end-user smoke test with WRITE operation
Maps strategy to CLI generation impact

evals.name: Novel GraphQL site (Linear.app)
evals.prompt: Plan the complete traffic capture and CLI implementation for https://linear.app — a project management tool. You have never seen this site before. Plan capture, analyze what protocol it likely uses, design the CLI, and explain how you'd handle the GraphQL API pattern if detected.

assertions.text: Runs framework detection eval commands before full capture
Identifies or hypothesizes GraphQL as the API protocol
Describes how to handle GraphQL in CLI generation (query templates or operations)
Proposes CLI command tree mapping GraphQL operations to commands
Uses Force SPA Navigation trick or explains why it may not be needed
Mentions read-only site escape hatch (if no mutations found)

evals.name: Novel streaming app (Perplexity.ai)
evals.prompt: Plan the complete traffic capture and CLI implementation for https://www.perplexity.ai — an AI search engine with streaming responses. You have never seen this site before. How would you capture the streaming API? How would the generated CLI handle Server-Sent Events (SSE)? What about the search + follow-up conversation pattern?

assertions.text: Identifies or discusses SSE/streaming response pattern
Explains how client.py would handle streaming responses
Identifies search as the primary WRITE operation
Runs site assessment before full capture
Plans auth handling (likely cookie or session-based)
Includes smoke test that verifies streaming search works
Proposes CLI command structure (search, follow-up, history)

evals.name: Novel read-only site (Product Hunt)
evals.prompt: Plan the traffic capture for https://www.producthunt.com — a product discovery site. Most features are read-only (browse products, view launches, search). The site may not have meaningful WRITE operations accessible to regular users. How do you handle the read-only escape hatch? Is this site CLI-suitable?

assertions.text: Acknowledges the site may be primarily read-only
Mentions the read-only escape hatch from the standards
Determines if the site IS CLI-suitable despite being read-only
Plans useful read-only CLI commands (search, list, get)
Identifies potential write operations (upvote, comment, follow) if any
Notes in APP.md that this is a read-only CLI
Adjusts validation expectations (no mandatory WRITE smoke test)
Proposes reasonable test strategy for a read-only CLI

evals.name: Novel Google batchexecute app (Google Keep)
evals.prompt: Plan the complete traffic capture and CLI for https://keep.google.com — Google's note-taking app. This uses the same batchexecute RPC protocol as NotebookLM. How would you detect it? What RPC codec structure would you build? How is auth different from REST APIs?

assertions.text: Detects or expects Google batchexecute protocol
Describes the rpc/ subpackage (encoder + decoder + types)
Plans WRITE operations (create note, edit, delete)
Discusses auth refresh pattern for Google (re-fetch tokens on 401)
